title: Great Men Have Been Among Us
content:
Great men have been among us; hands that penned
And tongues that uttered wisdom, better none:
The later Sidney, Marvel, Harrington,
Young Vane, and others who called Milton friend.
These moralists could act and comprehend:
They knew how genuine glory was put on;
Taught us how rightfully nation shone
In splendour: what strength was, that would not bend
But in magnanimous meekness. France, 'tis strange,
Hath brought forth no such souls as we had then.
Perpetual emptiness! unceasing change!
No single volume paramount, no code,
No master spirit, no determined road;
But equally a want of books and men!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Wordsworth delivers a powerful performance in "Great Men Have Been Among Us"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
